project_type: Java libGDX desktop game (roguelike dungeon crawler)
build_system: Gradle 8.11 with wrapper (./gradlew). Source in flat src/ directory (no src/main/java hierarchy). Single sourceSet configured in build.gradle with srcDirs=['src'].
test_framework: None - no test directory or test framework configured.
test_command: N/A - no tests exist. Verification is via build only.
verification_instructions: Run ./gradlew classes and confirm BUILD SUCCESSFUL. No unit tests exist.
setup_instructions: Java 17 is pre-configured via mise.toml. Gradle wrapper handles dependencies. Run ./gradlew classes to verify.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. No Docker needed. Build verification is ./gradlew classes only.
contribution_requirements: Commit to phase2-decouple-logic branch. Push via github Power push_to_remote.
key_patterns: Non-blocking state machine architecture (GameState enum drives all logic). Event-driven with GameEventDispatcher/GameEventListener. All sprites generated procedurally via Pixmap (SpriteGenerator.java). Music generated procedurally (MusicManager.java). Screens implement libGDX Screen interface + InputAdapter. CombatEngine is the central controller. SaveData uses manual JSON serialization (no libraries). 320x240 virtual resolution via PixelRenderer. All enemy classes extend Enemy which extends GameCharacter. Package structure: model/ (data), controller/ (logic), view/ (rendering/screens/sprites/audio).
directory_structure: src/ - flat package structure. Packages: controller/ (game logic, state), model/ (entities, items, skills, status, events, enemies), view/ (PlagueOfDanjinGame, screens/, sprites/, audio/, rendering/, effects/, assets/, ui/). Build output in build/. No test directory exists.
